---

## 🌟 Overview

**TButton Mod** revolutionizes how you manage cosmetics in Minecraft! With a sleek, modern dashboard accessible directly from the main menu, you can preview and manage your skins in stunning 3D. Whether you're on PC or mobile (PojavLauncher), this mod provides a seamless experience with customizable themes and real-time skin previews.

### ✨ Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| 🎯 **T Button** | Dedicated button on main menu for instant dashboard access |
| 🎨 **3D Skin Preview** | Full 3D Steve model with real-time skin rendering |
| 📱 **Cross-Platform** | Works perfectly on PC and PojavLauncher (Mobile) |
| 🎭 **Auto Folder Creation** | `TCosmetics/skins` folder created automatically |
| 🎪 **Theme System** | Customizable colors and GUI borders |
| ⚡ **Lightweight** | No external images - pure code-based rendering |
| 🖥️ **Responsive Design** | Adapts to different screen sizes |
| 🔧 **No Dependencies** | Works standalone with Fabric API |

## 🚀 Installation

### Prerequisites
- ✅ Minecraft **1.21**
- ✅ Fabric Loader **0.15.0+**
- ✅ Fabric API **0.96.0+**

### Manual Installation
1. Download the latest `.jar` file from [Releases](https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/tbuttonmod/releases)
2. Place it in your `.minecraft/mods` folder
3. Launch Minecraft with Fabric profile
4. Find the **T** button on the main menu!

### For PojavLauncher (Mobile)
1. Install PojavLauncher from [GitHub](https://github.com/PojavLauncherTeam/PojavLauncher)
2. Set up Minecraft 1.21 with Fabric
3. Copy the mod `.jar` to `internal_storage/games/pojavlauncher/.minecraft/mods/`
4. Launch and enjoy!

## ⚙️ Building from Source

### Using GitHub Actions (Automated)
1. Fork the repository
2. Go to **Actions** tab
3. Select **Build TButton Mod**
4. Click **Run workflow**
5. Download artifacts from completed workflow

### Manual Build
```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/tbuttonmod.git
cd tbuttonmod

# Make gradlew executable (Linux/Mac)
chmod +x gradlew

# Build the mod
./gradlew clean build

# Find the JAR in build/libs/
ls build/libs/
